heading: Crafting software that actually ships.
paragraphs: I'm a software engineer with 3+ years of experience building web applications and, increasingly, AI-powered products. I started with full-stack web development — React frontends, Node APIs, relational databases — and over the last couple of years have gone deep on the LLM tooling layer: embeddings, retrieval pipelines, evals, and the plumbing that makes AI features actually ship.

I'm drawn to roles where software craft and AI capability have to work together. Good abstractions still matter. Latency still matters. User experience still matters — maybe more so when the output is non-deterministic.
Outside of work I write about what I'm learning, contribute to open source, and try to read more than I tweet.
